questions.question: Does the timer-fire handler issue exactly one mlsGroup.commit() call (not two), combining all Remove proposals and the adminPubkeys update in a single extraProposals array? Two separate commit or proposeUpdateMetadata awaits are explicitly prohibited.
questions.verification_method: grep -n 'mlsGroup\.commit\|\.commit(' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx — the timer-fire function body must show at most one .commit( call; grep -n 'proposeUpdateMetadata' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx — must not appear as a standalone await call

questions.question: Does the timer callback re-fetch the live mlsGroup via clientRef.current?.groups.get(groupId) at fire time — not relying on a closed-over reference captured at enqueue time?
questions.verification_method: Read the timer-fire handler in MarmotContext.tsx — the very first action after the timer fires must be: const mlsGroup = clientRef.current?.groups.get(groupId); if (!mlsGroup) return;

questions.question: Could the auto-commit timer-fire logic be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside MarmotContext.tsx? If not, which cross-module dependencies exist and do they flow through declared seam contracts in architecture.md?
questions.verification_method: Read the timer-fire handler — imports used: getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes (from marmot-ts), Proposals.proposeUpdateMetadata (from marmot-ts), PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE (constant, from marmot-ts) — all declared library deps; no imports from handlers/ or registerHandlers.ts

questions.question: Does app/tests/unit/marmot/autoCommitLeave.test.ts exist and cover all three required assertions: (a) single-pubkey → one commit call with correct extraProposals shape; (b) empty getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes → commit not called and entry dropped; (c) two simultaneous pubkeys → one commit with two Remove proposals and correct remainingAdmins?
questions.verification_method: Read app/tests/unit/marmot/autoCommitLeave.test.ts — verify three it() blocks covering cases (a), (b), (c); run make test-unit and confirm all three pass

questions.question: Are Remove proposals built as plain objects { proposalType: PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE, remove: { removed: leafIndex } } — not using proposeRemoveUser() or any Proposals.Remove(...) factory?
questions.verification_method: grep -n 'proposeRemoveUser\|Proposals\.Remove' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx — must return empty; grep -n 'PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx — must return at least 1 match in the timer-fire handler

questions.question: On commit failure (exception), does the timer handler leave the pending-removal entries in the queue for retry (not discard them), and does it NOT retry immediately with the cached removeProposals array?
questions.verification_method: Read the catch block in the timer-fire handler — must NOT clear the pending entries for the failed commit; must NOT call commit again inside the catch block; on next timer tick getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes will re-derive leaf indexes

questions.question: When the debounce timer fires, does the callback re-fetch mlsGroup via clientRef.current?.groups.get(groupId), and return without committing (leaving queue entries in place) if mlsGroup is null?
questions.verification_method: Read timer-fire handler — first line must be mlsGroup re-fetch via clientRef; early-return on null must precede any queue mutation or commit attempt

questions.question: For each pending pubkey, does the timer callback call getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes(mlsGroup.state, pubkey), and drop the entry from the queue (no Remove proposal built) when the returned array is empty?
questions.verification_method: Read the per-pubkey loop in the timer-fire handler — verify getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes call and the if (indexes.length === 0) drop-and-continue branch; run unit test case (b) from autoCommitLeave.test.ts

questions.question: Are Remove proposals built as plain objects { proposalType: PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE, remove: { removed: leafIndex } } with PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE = 3, without using proposeRemoveUser() or Proposals.Remove(...)?
questions.verification_method: Read the proposal-building section in the timer-fire handler; grep -n 'proposeRemoveUser\|Proposals\.Remove' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx — must return empty; verify PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE === 3 in the import or constant declaration

questions.question: Does the timer callback issue exactly one mlsGroup.commit({ extraProposals: [...removeProposals, Proposals.proposeUpdateMetadata({ adminPubkeys: remainingAdmins })] }) call combining all Remove proposals and the metadata update?
questions.verification_method: grep -c '\.commit(' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx — count inside the timer-fire function must be exactly 1; read the commit call and verify extraProposals includes both removeProposals spread and proposeUpdateMetadata

questions.question: Is remainingAdmins computed as mlsGroup.groupData?.adminPubkeys.filter(pk => !departingPubkeys.includes(pk)) at timer-fire time from the live group object (not a stale closure)?
questions.verification_method: Read the remainingAdmins derivation in the timer-fire handler — must access mlsGroup.groupData?.adminPubkeys (from the re-fetched live mlsGroup) and filter out the departing pubkeys at the point of commit, not at enqueue time

questions.question: On successful commit, does the callback remove from pendingRemovalsRef only the entries for the pubkeys just committed, leaving entries added during the commit window in place for the next timer cycle?
questions.verification_method: Read the post-commit success path in the timer-fire handler — must filter pendingRemovalsRef.current.get(groupId) to keep entries not in the committed set, not clear the entire array

questions.question: On commit failure, do the failing entries remain in pendingRemovalsRef without an immediate retry using the cached removeProposals array?
questions.verification_method: Read the catch block — must NOT clear or modify pendingRemovalsRef on failure; must NOT call commit again; entries stay for the next timer tick's getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes re-derivation

questions.question: Does app/tests/unit/marmot/autoCommitLeave.test.ts assert all three cases: (a) single-pubkey → one commit with correct extraProposals shape; (b) getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes returns [] → commit not called and entry dropped; (c) two simultaneous pubkeys → one commit with two Remove proposals and correct remainingAdmins?
questions.verification_method: Read autoCommitLeave.test.ts and verify all three it() blocks are present with the named inputs; run make test-unit to confirm all three pass

questions.question: When leaveHandler.ts receives a kind-13 rumor for a pubkey whose getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes returns [] (already removed), is enqueueLeave still called (the stale-leaf guard is at commit time, not at enqueue time), and does the timer-fire silently drop the entry without entering an error state?
questions.verification_method: Read leaveHandler.ts handle() — enqueueLeave must be called on any valid kind-13 rumor regardless of ratchet-tree state; read timer-fire handler — getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes empty → drop silently with no throw or error toast

questions.question: When a losing admin's commit is rolled back after a dual-admin race, does the pending-queue re-check (getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes returns [] after rollback) prevent a second Remove commit from being issued?
questions.verification_method: Read the timer-fire handler — the stale-leaf guard (getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes returns [] → drop) is the sole defense; verify there is no additional first-wins deduplication that might accidentally bypass the guard; run unit test case (b) from autoCommitLeave.test.ts

questions.question: When multiple members leave simultaneously within the 5-second debounce window, are their pubkeys batched into a single mlsGroup.commit call with multiple Remove proposals in one extraProposals array?
questions.verification_method: Read the timer-fire handler — pendingRemovalsRef.current.get(groupId) must iterate over all pending pubkeys and accumulate removeProposals before the single commit call; run unit test case (c) from autoCommitLeave.test.ts

questions.question: When group.commit succeeds locally but publish fails, do the pending-removal entries remain in pendingRemovalsRef, and does the retry path re-derive leaf indexes via getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes rather than using the cached removeProposals array from the failed attempt?
questions.verification_method: Read the catch block in the timer-fire handler — confirm entries are NOT cleared on publish failure; confirm the timer will re-arm (or will re-fire on next enqueueLeave), re-calling getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes freshly

questions.question: On admin cold-start, are kind-13 messages re-delivered through historical sync routed through leaveHandler.ts via the dispatcher, repopulating the pending-removal queue, with the 5-second debounce absorbing the burst into a single commit per group?
questions.verification_method: Read the dispatcher wiring in registerHandlers.ts and MarmotContext's subscribeToGroupMessages call — confirm historical messages go through the same buildDispatcher path; the 5s debounce reset-on-new-enqueue behavior in enqueueLeave is the burst absorber

questions.question: When an auto-remove commit is rolled back by a concurrent chat or poll commit targeting the same epoch, do the pending-removal queue entries remain and does the auto-remove logic re-fire on the next timer tick with fresh getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes derivation?
questions.verification_method: Read the catch block — commit rollback (exception) must leave entries in pendingRemovalsRef; verify no immediate retry inside catch; the next timer tick (or next enqueueLeave reset) will re-derive leaf indexes

questions.question: fireAutoCommit calls onCommitted for stale-leaf drops (empty leafIndexes) eagerly, BEFORE the mlsGroup.commit() call. Does this introduce a window where the stale entry is removed from the live queue but the commit for valid entries subsequently fails — leaving the queue in a partially cleaned state that prevents re-delivery of the stale pubkey on the next cycle?
questions.verification_method: Read fireAutoCommit: onCommitted([droppedPubkeys]) is called unconditionally for stale entries even when the subsequent commit throws. Since these pubkeys returned [] from getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes they are already removed from the ratchet — no Remove commit is needed for them. Dropping them from the queue on failure is correct: they cannot be committed. Verify by the 'mixed stale + valid' test case in autoCommitLeave.test.ts.

questions.question: The test file mocks React via vi.mock('react') to allow importing MarmotContext.tsx in a non-DOM environment. If React's API shape changes (e.g. useRef returns a different shape), the mock could silently mask import-time failures. Does the current mock adequately guard against MarmotContext.tsx import errors in the test environment?
questions.verification_method: Run make test-unit and confirm autoCommitLeave.test.ts imports succeed and all 6 tests pass. Review the vi.mock('react') mock in autoCommitLeave.test.ts — it returns stable stubs for all hooks called at MarmotProvider component scope, not at module scope. fireAutoCommit is module-scope (not inside the component), so the React mock only needs to prevent component-body execution at import time.

questions.question: The timer callback in enqueueLeave uses a void IIFE (void (async () => { ... })()) to wrap the async auto-commit body. If the outer try/catch is somehow bypassed (e.g. synchronous throw before the async import), could an unhandled rejection leak through?
questions.verification_method: Read the timer body in enqueueLeave: the void IIFE wraps the entire body. The mlsGroup re-fetch is guarded with its own try/catch. The dynamic import and fireAutoCommit call are inside the outer try/catch. There is no code path between 'pendingQueue.length === 0 return' and the outer try/catch that could throw synchronously without being caught.
